messages.content:
Defining a clear problem statement can help you avoid this common pitfall.
Select an answer:
focusing on the unimportant
jumping to answers too quickly
not thinking of future consequences
generating weak hypotheses
focusing on the unimportant

What does a problem statement define, overall?
Select an answer:
what success for the project looks like
what role each stakeholder will take
the tasks involved in solving the problem
the symptoms of the problem
what success for the project looks like

Three different lenses for thinking critically are to change your _____, to change the context, or to change the reality of the problem space.
Select an answer:
point of view
objectives
project statement
feelings
point of view

The "7 So What's" are a critical tool for analyzing _____.
Select an answer:
consequences of recommendations
causes underlying problems
probability of milestones
roots of origin
consequences of recommendations

If you want to improve your team's critical thinking skills you'll need to introduce them to the tools, give them opportunities to practice, coach them along the way, and _____.
Select an answer:
encourage them to rate each other's work
hold them accountable when they don't apply the methods
teach them to believe in themselves
reward the best performing team member
encourage them to apply the tools in real-world situations

You just completed your analysis for a project and have your initial recommendation completed. After validating your results, what is the best next step you should take?
Select an answer:
submit the results of the project to your manager for review
implement your ideas immediately
compare your results with other unrelated projects to see if new insights emerge
make sure your results are plausible
make sure your results are plausible

Comparing the solutions from _____ can improve your analysis and lead to the discovery of new kinds of solutions.
Select an answer:
high-level projects
successful projects
unauthorized projects
unrelated projects
unrelated projects

What is the best tool for getting to a problem's root cause?
Select an answer:
the 7 So What's
the 3 How's
the 5 Forces
the 5 Why's
the 5 Why's

Vilfredo Pareto, the author of the 80/20 rule, observed this rule to be in effect in both _____ and _____.
Select an answer:
fishing; the stock market
real estate; gardening
gardening; spelunking
eating; blinking
real estate; gardening

You have a very efficient and reliable team member on your team, but they are infamous for treating symptoms rather than root causes. What is the likely outcome of using their quick assistance?
Select an answer:
Their output will be undocumented and unreliable.
The team may be provoked into disagreement.
The project will be reliable for years to come.
Any fix provided will be short-lived.
Any fix provided will be short-lived.

What's the most productive way to apply the 80/20 principle to critical thinking?
Select an answer:
Focus on the efforts that impact 20% of the results.
Spend 80% of your effort analyzing causes, and 20% analyzing consequences.
Focus on the 20% of efforts that impact 80% of the results.
20% of your efforts are unnecessary and should be cut.
Focus on the 20% of efforts that impact 80% of the results.

You have a team member who is a bit inflexible; they are prone to doing their job without much reflection and they do not like change. Which pitfalls likely impact their critical thinking?
Select an answer:
being unwilling to change the problem space, and failing to consider implications
failing to consider similar situations in unrelated areas, and not understanding the fundamental causes
focusing on things that do not matter, and not using the 80/20 rule
jumping to answers too quickly, and not teaching others their methods
being unwilling to change the problem space, and failing to consider implications

What is a side-benefit of involving multiple team members in the ideation process?
Select an answer:
It creates a sense of shared ownership of the solution.
Each team member learns new design skills.
It ensures that everyone is kept busy during this phase of the process.
It gives insight into how each team member thinks.
It creates a sense of shared ownership of the solution.

Your company's current product line is mature, and you are looking to expand into new areas. Which user-centered approach is most useful?
Select an answer:
content testing
usability testing
information architecture research
field research and ethnography
field research and ethnography

How can you best demonstrate the success of your design thinking process to your executives?
Select an answer:
Run a team member satisfaction survey.
Ask some customers what they think of the new product when it's released.
Show the financial impact of each of your metrics.
The results of the project should speak for themselves.
Show the financial impact of each of your metrics.

What is a central benefit of design thinking?
Select an answer:
Business and technology team members won't have to work together.
You can bypass the technology-centric individuals on the team and focus just on the more creative ones.
Business representatives can dictate the approach that will be taken.
It helps technology-centric individuals understand customer issues.
It helps technology-centric individuals understand customer issues.

What might best persuade people from different disciplines that it's worth their while to attend the design thinking exercise?
Select an answer:
Persuade their managers to force them to attend.
Tell them there will be donuts.
Let them know that being involved now lets everyone have their say.
Explain that the choice is to spend some concentrated planning time up front, or multiple meetings later on.
Explain that the choice is to spend some concentrated planning time up front, or multiple meetings later on.

What is the planning output from the design thinking process?
Select an answer:
a step-by-step plan for building out each section of the product in turn
a set of interface sketches that the development team can use to create a story backlog
an incremental plan that delivers core functionality early, adding functionality in frequent iterative releases
a Gantt chart with time and resource estimates for each feature you plan to implement
an incremental plan that delivers core functionality early, adding functionality in frequent iterative releases

What's the best way to sell design thinking to influential individuals in your organization?
Select an answer:
Meet one-on-one and focus on the one benefit of the process that most helps each individual.
Send out a meeting request and hope that everyone shows up.
Bring them all into a room together for a three-hour presentation on the benefits of design thinking.
Call them on the telephone and tell them you need their involvement.
Meet one-on-one and focus on the one benefit of the process that most helps each individual.

How does design thinking help dispersed or outsourced teams?
Select an answer:
Team members can each submit their own ideas of how the design should work.
The artifacts paint a picture of the customer interaction so every team member starts from the same understanding.
Design thinking requires frequent check-ins so everyone communicates better.
Because design thinking happens so fast, nobody has a chance to question the decisions.
The artifacts paint a picture of the customer interaction so every team member starts from the same understanding.

What does the user testing and feedback part of design thinking give you?
Select an answer:
an opportunity to test out marketing messages you might use for your new product
a chance to ask users questions about your current products.
confidence that your concepts are likely to meet users' needs
a chance to justify your changes to users
confidence that your concepts are likely to meet users' needs

What does an iterative development approach help you to do?
Select an answer:
Split the work up into more manageable chunks.
Decide what's best on behalf of customers, so you don't have to build so much.
Identify the most valuable pieces to implement to maximize business and customer benefit.
Spread the work over more releases in case the project gets canceled halfway through.
Identify the most valuable pieces to implement to maximize business and customer benefit.
